meaning: n.(西非的)班巴拉族(人),班巴拉人所用之曼丁哥语
examples: She brought writers such as Angela Davis and Toni Cade Bambara to national prominence.
Mali has several tribes, the largest of which are the Bambara who account for more than 30 percent of the population and whose language is widely spoken.
The seeds, weighing 330kg (730lb), are made up of varieties of domesticated and wild cowpea, maize, soybean and bambara groundnut.
Most of the manuscripts are in Arabic and are from the 14th to 16th Centuries, but many are written in local languages, including Songhai, Tamashek and Bambara.
